cards.back: A process of selecting a sample from a population in such a way that every individual in the population has an equal chance of being selected
A type of bias that occurs when a sample is selected in a way that is not representative of the population
A distribution of the sample statistics that would result from repeated sampling from a population with the same sampling method
A measure of the dispersion of the population data, calculated as the average of the squared differences from the mean
A measure of the dispersion of the sample data, calculated as the average of the squared differences from the sample mean
